provenance.transformation: The active Codex Agent manually paraphrased selected evidence after runtime redaction. Project names, account names, paths, raw session identifiers, and verbatim private transcripts were omitted.
provenance.claim_boundary: This is a small product case study, not a precision/recall benchmark or evidence of causal improvement.

cases.title: Forked logs inflated one collaboration session
evidence: Fourteen recent rollout files represented six independent sessions; eight inherited fork or snapshot files repeated a parent session.
The same user turn was commonly serialized as adjacent response-item and event-message records; 210 duplicates were collapsed.
The longest retained session exposed 2,249 events. The bounded capsule disclosed that it sampled 160 and omitted 2,089.
cases.agent_judgment: The evidence substrate was overweighting one long collaboration and allowing tool traffic to crowd out user intent. That is a runtime evidence-quality defect, not a semantic friction judgment.
cases.recommended_outcome: PATCH / HARNESS: collapse inherited session files, deduplicate dual user-message serialization, accept only explicit user-message sources, and disclose bounded sampling.

cases.title: Invisible scheduling caused friction in two projects
evidence: In one project, the user asked where scheduling and debug status were and whether the recurring process could be started manually.
In a later project, the user asked for a default reminder to run a review before the weekly usage reset.
cases.agent_judgment: The recurring problem was discoverability and trust, not the absence of another scheduler implementation. Codex already provides a visible Scheduled inbox and can invoke a Skill on a cadence.
cases.recommended_outcome: PATCH / SKILL: after the first user-initiated review, offer an opt-in native Scheduled task that may stage findings but can never apply them.

cases.title: The user repeatedly requested a plain-language explanation
evidence: During a design review, the user asked for four technical fixes to be explained again in plain language.
In a separate learning project, the user said the Agent had completed work without explaining what the workflow was teaching or why the steps mattered.
cases.agent_judgment: The pattern recurred across independent projects, but the existing global collaboration guidance already requires plain language plus purpose, impact, and risk before state-changing work. Adding another prose rule would duplicate an existing capability.
cases.recommended_outcome: NO CHANGE / REUSE: keep the existing guidance, surface this as an adherence problem, and collect later evidence before proposing a mechanical gate.
